items.title: The Harrowing Journey Home for Families Leaving Immigration Detention
items.description: Parents and children from a detention center in Texas found themselves dropped at a border town shelter with few means to leave.

items.title: As Texas Restricts Cashless Bail, More People Will Be Jailed for Months Based on an Accusation
items.description: Despite evidence that cashless bail doesn’t increase crime, several states are moving to restrict it.

items.title: The Documentary ‘Exodus’ Paints a Nuanced Picture of Life After Lockup
items.description: Shot over two years, the Critics’ Choice-nominated film values quiet moments. “We’re trying to refuse a spectacle,” says director Nimco Sheikhaden.

items.title: Mississippi’s Black Voters Brace for Elections Ruling That Could Gut Supreme Court Clout
items.description: Black Mississippians won a Voting Rights Act case that challenges how the state elects supreme court justices. But that victory may be in jeopardy.

items.title: Beating by Guards, Not a Heart Attack, Killed Man in Mississippi Prison, Report Shows
items.description: FBI is now investigating after a report showed that Mississippi guards beat an incarcerated person to death.
